semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all fourteen exact source mappings, the schema and four-valid/eight-invalid deterministic trace, all three proof targets, all eleven SearchSubstrates theorem declarations, every manifest atom, every prose candidate, test plan, source crosswalk, JEPA boundary, summary, and handoff. The sweep made the consumer-, use-, workload-, claim-axis-, implementation-, baseline-, resource-, and time-specific Substrate Adoption Lease the distinct owner; expanded it to eighteen lifecycle mechanisms, twelve interfaces, eighteen invariants, and eighteen failure modes; preserved the exact record-only evidence boundary; and froze the natural candidate, matched-baseline, joint-burden, causal, independent-reproduction, and heterogeneous-transfer campaign with zero support-state effect.

scope.population: Named consumers of versioned calculus, representation, recurrence, search, latent-prediction, state-space, sequence, graph, compiler, cyclic, and geometric substrate candidates on declared natural and adversarial workloads.
scope.environment: Fourteen exact mappings; one schema-valid record; four valid synthetic adoption states; eight expected-invalid controls; three proof targets; eleven finite theorem declarations. No candidate substrate implementation, learned model, kernel A/B, natural workload, measured benefit, independent reproduction, or transfer result is represented.
scope.model: A consumer-, use-, workload-, claim-axis-, implementation-, baseline-, resource-, environment-, and time-specific lease over registration, structural receipts, matched experiments, axis ledgers, total burden, canary permission, fallback, retirement, reproduction, transfer, expiry, and residuals.
scope.authority: This chapter owns candidate-to-consumer substrate adoption permission only. Intent, Planning, Compilation, Context, Representation, Routing, Generation, Deliberation, Resources, Verification, Proof, Security, Privacy, Rights, Runtime, Artifacts, Readiness, Incident, Release, Claims, and Evidence retain their boundaries.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-14; material substrate, implementation, theorem, workload, baseline, model, hardware, data, evaluator, policy, rights, threat, consumer, organization, or time changes expire affected leases.
scope.artifact: Chapter and manifest contract; fourteen mappings; protocol fixture; deterministic trace; three proof targets; eleven declarations; semantic review; and future natural causal-transfer campaign.

assumptions: Candidate identity, implementation, mathematical object, consumer, use, workload, environment, claim axes, baselines, resources, rights, expiry, and residuals can be versioned without collapsing their owners.

Inspectable candidates and executable ordinary, strongest-current, simple-ablation, and embarrassing controls can run with matched information, resources, hardware, tuning, verifier access, retries, and evaluator opportunity.

Independent-enough implementations and evaluators can measure structural validity, fidelity/rate, search, routing, task utility, latency, memory, energy, verifier and operator burden, safety, rights, fallback, recovery, and residuals.
Failures, refusals, timeouts, OOMs, fallbacks, repairs, and unscorable cases remain in complete denominators.
Causal interventions, independent reproduction, and heterogeneous transfer can be frozen prospectively.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: Ordinary Transformer, recurrent, state-space, retrieval, graph, compiler, literal-search, and standard numerical methods already dominate; the adoption exchange adds bookkeeping and narrative space for exotic substrates without unique useful benefit.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: Under matched natural workloads, no candidate yields its preregistered consumer-specific causal signature and joint fidelity, utility, resource, verifier, safety, rights, fallback, and recovery frontier; or weak baselines, outcome-driven selection, hidden search cost, denominator censorship, evaluator coupling, failed reproduction, or failed transfer explains the result.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze strongest current recurrent, state-space, graph, search, latent-prediction, and ordinary comparators without importing reported gains.
Justify least-authority consumer gates, rights boundaries, retirement, finite optionality, and residual ownership.
Specify semantic correspondence, transition systems, countermodels, liveness, fallback, recovery, and runtime refinement beyond finite record projections.
Implement candidates, matched controls, natural workloads, attacks, fallback, quarantine, rollback, and complete instrumentation.
Measure every claim axis and total training, inference, search, verification, human, fallback, recovery, and opportunity burden jointly.
Ablate the claimed cyclic, graph, recurrent, selective-state, latent-prediction, adaptive-halting, or search mechanism under matched budgets.
Reproduce independently and transfer across consumers, workloads, models, scales, hardware, data, organizations, rights regimes, attacks, and time.

contrary_evidence: The current evidence validates record discipline and synthetic rejection controls, not a substrate implementation or benefit.
The four valid trace states and eight invalid controls were authored for the schema boundary and are not natural workload observations.
All source-reported architecture and benchmark results remain external, source-reported, speculative, or local-project comparator context.

Eleven theorem declarations are three direct record projections, five derived negatives, and three authored fixture consequences; none proves implementation correspondence, usefulness, fallback liveness, recovery, reproduction, or transfer.
No support-state transition, candidate qualification, substrate-core adoption, deployment, or SOTA result exists.
known_confounds: Candidate and baseline implementation quality, tuning, kernels, caches, hardware, data, sequence length, scale, search budget, and warmup can dominate results.
Outcome-driven selection across candidates, seeds, workloads, metrics, and budgets can create false discovery.
Contamination, shared evaluators, shared implementation defects, or non-independent data can create false agreement.
Favorable averages can hide tails, subgroups, pathological structures, verifier burden, rights failures, and downstream regressions.
Short horizons can hide maintenance, fallback, recovery, drift, lock-in, and opportunity costs.
acceptance_criteria: Every lifecycle stage has an exact producer, consumer, valid case, rejecting mutation, consequence, recovery route, and residual owner.
At least one inspectable candidate and all matched controls execute natural bounded work with complete axis and total-burden measurement, failure, fallback, recovery, and public-safe artifacts.
The claimed mechanism produces its preregistered signature under matched causal intervention and clears the consumer-specific joint frontier.
An independent implementation and evaluator reproduce the result, and heterogeneous transfer passes without hidden axis or rights regressions.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact substrate and implementation version, consumer, use, workload, environment, axis, baseline, resource budget, evaluator, rights regime, and horizon.
Separate structural proof, implementation refinement, schema validity, synthetic trace, model quality, search, routing, representation, resources, safety, deployment, transfer, and SOTA.
Unknown denominators, total burden, independence, fallback, recovery, reproduction, transfer, or rights remain residual or blocked.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable trace permits axis laundering, baseline mismatch, denominator censorship, unavailable fallback, stale lease, rights violation, or unowned residual.
Refute a mechanism benefit when matched ablation preserves or improves the joint frontier or weak baselines, extra resources, selection, leakage, or shared evaluation explain it.
Refute useful, efficient, safe, general, deployable, transferable, or SOTA language when a simpler control wins or independent reproduction and transfer fail.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise adoption owner supersedes the atom and every lease, proof receipt, axis ledger, run, fallback, transition, incident, residual, and descendant migrates with lineage.

reproduction.command: python3 scripts/validate_substrate_adoption_trace.py && cd lean && lake build AsiStackProofs.SearchSubstrates
reproduction.environment_lock: Local schema fixture, deterministic synthetic trace, and finite Lean module current through 2026-07-14; natural candidate campaign not frozen or run.
reproduction.evaluator_identity: Current validator and formal checks are local authored implementations; independent candidate, workload, evaluator, causal, reproduction, and transfer implementations are required.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze and run inspectable substrate candidates on natural bounded workloads with strong matched controls, joint total-burden metrics, causal ablations, executable fallback and recovery, independent implementation/evaluation, and heterogeneous transfer.

non_claims: A source, theorem, schema, fixture, deterministic trace, validator, or green build does not establish substrate usefulness, model quality, efficiency, safety, deployment, transfer, or SOTA.
No assigned source result is reproduced or imported as an ASI Stack result.
No support, deployment, release, AGI, or ASI state changes through this semantic review.

mathematical-and-search-substrates.core.strongest_counterclaim: Ordinary methods dominate and the exchange merely shelters exotic mechanisms.
mathematical-and-search-substrates.core.falsifier: Matched natural causal campaigns show no unique consumer-specific joint-frontier benefit or fail fallback, reproduction, transfer, or rights gates.
